stories.title: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO
stories.summary: OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, filed confidentially for an IPO, joining artificial intelligence rivals in tapping public markets to fund ambitious growth plans. Bloomberg's Michael Hytha has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO as Rivals Race to Market
stories.summary: OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, filed confidentially for an IPO, joining artificial intelligence rivals in tapping public markets to fund ambitious growth plans.

stories.title: Perplexity plans IPO in 2028 regardless of what happens to Anthropic or OpenAI, CEO tells CNBC
stories.summary: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas' comments come after AI giant Anthropic confidentially filed to go public.

stories.title: Xi Receives Lavish Welcome From Kim in North Korea
stories.summary: President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang to an elaborate welcome on his first visit to the reclusive nation in seven years In a meeting with Kim Jong Un, Xi vowed to deepen China's ties with North Korea. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Apollo and Blackstone raise $35bn in chip financing deal for Anthropic
stories.summary: Transaction is one of the largest private credit fundraisings, fuelling the Claude maker’s AI growth plans

stories.title: OpenAI plans to go public, intensifying investment race with Anthropic
stories.summary: The company behind ChatGPT filed its plans one week after Anthropic did the same.

stories.title: Jim Cramer says sovereign AI is Nvidia's powerful new growth driver
stories.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said Nvidia's growing sovereign AI business could help reduce the company's dependence on hyperscalers.

stories.title: Elon Musk Shows Detailed Design of AI Data Center Satellite
stories.summary: Elon Musk unveiled a more detailed look at an initial version of an AI data center satellite SpaceX plans to build, providing fresh insight into the ambitious project driving the company’s highly anticipated initial public offering.

stories.title: Former Amb. Taylor: Ukraine is Winning the War
stories.summary: Former US Ambassador to Ukraine William Taylor says Ukraine is winning the war, pointing to battlefield gains, deeper strikes inside Russia and pressure on Vladimir Putin's economy. He also discusses US support for Kyiv and calls for stronger weapons, sanctions and backing for Ukraine with Kailey Leinz and Joe Mathieu on Bloomberg's "Balance of Power." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Rep. Krishnamoorthi: FISA at Risk Over Pulte Pick
stories.summary: Democratic Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi of Illinois says President Trump should pull Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence to save FISA, warning he does not see Democrats backing reauthorization if Pulte remains in the position. The House Intelligence Committee member also discusses OpenAI, AI regulation, US-China tech competition and the Iran conflict with Kailey Leinz and Joe Mathie

stories.title: OpenAI files to go public in blockbuster Wall Street listing
stories.summary: ChatGPT creator has submitted paperwork for IPO expected to value company at more than $1tn

stories.title: OpenAI confidentially files for IPO, prepping Wall Street for mega AI debut
stories.summary: OpenAI's confidential filing lands days before SpaceX is set to go public and a week after Anthropic announced its confidential disclosure with the SEC.

stories.title: Apple partnering with Google and Nvidia for most advanced AI model
stories.summary: Apple's artificial intelligence strategy took center stage at its Worldwide Developers Conference in Cupertino, Calif.

stories.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declines Senate testimony on AI, China and exports
stories.summary: Sen. Elizabeth Warren said Nvidia’s CEO should answer questions publicly as lawmakers scrutinize AI chip sales to China and export controls.

stories.summary: Today, Ed Ludlow from Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference in Cupertino, as the tech giant announces a reworked AI slate, including a revamped Siri. Then, LinkedIn head of economics, Americas Kory Kantenga, discusses the labor market as businesses and new grads feels the impact of AI. Plus, author and podcast host Ruthie Rogers talks food culture and her new book 'Table 4 at the River Cafe.' Al

stories.title: Corning strikes another multibillion-dollar AI deal. What the new Amazon pact means for the stock
stories.summary: Amazon joins the laundry list of partnerships Corning has secured in 2026, which includes Meta Platforms and Nvidia.

stories.title: Wall Street rebounds as traders race back into chip stocks
stories.summary: Intel and Micron among big gainers as fervour over AI-linked shares resumes

stories.title: Gold Steadies After Israel and Iran Agree to End Missile Strikes
stories.summary: Gold was steady after Israel and Iran agreed to end attacks that had jeopardized talks to end the war in the Middle East.

stories.title: Singapore Brings Back 19th-Century Tech to Beat Warming Climate
stories.summary: Deep underneath Singapore’s northeastern district of Punggol, a five-kilometer network of metal pipes roars as it pumps chilled water to cool offices and classrooms overhead.

stories.title: Oil Steady as Israel and Iran Halt Hostilities That Risked Talks
stories.summary: Oil steadied after Israel and Iran agreed to end attacks against each other following an escalation of violence that threatened to derail efforts to end the war in the Middle East.

stories.title: Vale CEO Says Metals Demand Is Robust Despite Conflict With Iran
stories.summary: Gustavo Pimenta, CEO of Vale, says that conflict with Iran has not impacted global demand for metals from the top iron ore producer. Pimenta also discussed the impact of the war on its sites in Oman, saying that he remains optimistic about the facility's future despite having to shut down production during the conflict. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: The Growing Population of Zombie ETF
stories.summary: Bloomberg Intelligence's Athanasios Psarofagis joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the growing population of zombie ETFs, which are defined as funds that are at least three years old and manage less than $50 million. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: NZ Commits to First LNG Import Plant Despite Gas Price Surge
stories.summary: The New Zealand government will take the next step to build the country’s first liquefied natural gas import facility, sticking with its plan to bolster energy security despite the surge in global gas prices due to the Iran war.

stories.title: Vale CEO Sees No Demand Destruction in Metals Markets
stories.summary: Vale SA Chief Executive Officer Gustavo Pimenta says he sees no evidence of demand destruction in global metal markets despite war in the Middle East. In fact, he sees growth in demand around the world. He speaks to Bloomberg's Lisa Abramowicz. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Iran, Israel Pledge to End Attacks | Balance of Power 6/8/2026
stories.summary: On the early edition of Balance of Power, Bloomberg Washington Correspondents Joe Mathieu and Kailey Leinz discuss the latest news from the Trump Administration. stories.title: Convicted FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried files formal request for Trump pardon
stories.summary: The co-founder of the now defunct FTX crypto exchange is currently serving a 25-year federal prison sentence.

stories.title: UN Says Children Are Dying in Cuba Because of Strict US Sanctions
stories.summary: Tighter US sanctions on Cuba have led to a spike in infant mortality and plummeting survival rates for child cancer patients, the United Nations warned in one of its strongest rebukes of Washington’s pressure campaign against the island.

stories.title: Jailed crypto founder Sam Bankman-Fried seeks Trump pardon
stories.summary: The former leader of crypto platform FTX, currently serving a 25-year sentence, on Monday officially applied to be pardoned.

stories.title: Climate Change, Pollution Push Oceans to Tipping Point, UN Report Says
stories.summary: Dying coral reefs and depleted fish stocks are signs of the “grave risk” to marine health, according to the third World Ocean Assessment.

stories.title: Cipher Sells Junk Debt for Amazon-Tied Data Center Project
stories.summary: Cipher Digital Inc. raised $810 million from a junk-bond sale to help fund a data center tied to Amazon.com Inc., adding to a wave of riskier debt financing backing the artificial intelligence infrastructure boom.

stories.title: Corning shares jump 4% after company strikes deal to power Amazon AI data centers in U.S.
stories.summary: Amazon is the latest megacap company to announce a massive deal with Corning, which is rapidly becoming a critical player in the AI buildout.

stories.title: FTX Co-Founder Sam Bankman-Fried Applies for Trump Pardon
stories.summary: FTX co-founder Sam Bankman-Fried formally applied for a presidential pardon, more than two years after he was convicted over the multi-billion dollar collapse of his once-thriving cryptocurrency empire.

stories.title: Marvell Technology jumps 10% after news it will join the S&P 500 index
stories.summary: Marvell stock jumped after S&P Global said the AI chipmaker would join the S&P 500 on June 22.

stories.summary: A deal will rank as one of UK drugmaker’s biggest as biotech M&A activity has enjoyed an upswing this year

stories.summary: In my travels around the world, I’m often asked if the best days of Hong Kong are behind us. My answer is always an emphatic “no” – and this is not merely a pep talk. When you step back and take a broad macroeconomic view, it becomes clear that, structurally speaking, our greatest opportunities lie just ahead. While many commentators scramble to draw parallels between Japan’s “lost decades” and Ch

stories.title: Pentagon expands list of China military-linked firms to include Alibaba, Baidu in fresh blow to diplomatic thaw
stories.summary: The Pentagon briefly posted a similar expanded list in February, then withdrew it without explanation before Trump's China trip.

stories.title: MiniMax once led Zhipu in Hong Kong’s AI stock race. How the tables have turned
stories.summary: When Chinese artificial intelligence developers Zhipu AI and MiniMax made their debuts on the Hong Kong stock exchange in January, investors initially saw one as a better bet than the other. Beijing-based Zhipu closed its first trading day on January 8 with a market capitalisation of HK$57.9 billion (US$7.4 billion). A day later, Shanghai-based MiniMax listed at HK$106.7 billion, almost twice as l

stories.title: Michigan, Canada Plan Opening of Bridge Trump Threatened to Block
stories.summary: Officials in the US and Canada are planning a ribbon-cutting ceremony in the coming days for a new C$6.4 billion ($4.6 billion) bridge linking Michigan to the province of Ontario, which President Donald Trump threatened to block earlier this year.

stories.title: UK to Repay Visa Fees to Growth Firms Which Recruit Abroad
stories.summary: The UK government will reimburse visa fees incurred by fast-growing companies when they recruit talent from abroad, in an effort to make Britain a friendlier environment for technology firms.

stories.title: WestJet Slams Canada’s Loan Offer to Airlines Hit by Fuel Costs
stories.summary: WestJet Airlines Ltd. condemned the Canadian government’s offer of loans to help carriers hit by high jet fuel costs in the wake of the closing of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Treasury Asked to Clarify Fast-Growing ETF Tax-Busting Strategy
stories.summary: The Investment Company Institute has asked the US Treasury Department for guidance on a fast-growing ETF strategy that allows investors to sidestep immediate capital-gains taxes, as uncertainty grows over how regulators view the practice.

stories.title: Asian Stocks Stage Cautious Recovery, Oil Slips: Markets Wrap

stories.title: Ingredion CEO Says Tate & Lyle Deal Creates Powerhouse
stories.summary: Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), and will end the UK company’s near-century listing on the London Stock Exchange. Ingredion Chief Executive Officer James Zallie says the US company paid a "fair" premium and this deal creates an ingredients "powerhouse." He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: SpaceX IPO Forces Investors to Bet on Musk’s Entangled AI Empire
stories.summary: The boundaries between Elon Musk’s companies are growing increasingly blurred through shared capital, talent and infrastructure. Investors must decide what that ecosystem is worth.

stories.title: Trump’s Latest Midterms Problem Is an Emboldened Iran

stories.title: New York’s Penn Station Rehab Eyes Billions in Federal Funding
stories.summary: The entities transforming New York’s Penn Station are slated to learn how much federal money will be directed to the project in about six months, according to Peter Cipriano, chief executive officer of the Halmar-Skanska consortium, the renovation’s master developer.

stories.title: Apple Delays Siri AI for iPhone Users in the EU, Says Regulators Refusing to Engage
stories.summary: Apple Inc. said it isn’t currently able to launch Siri AI, its redesigned digital assistant, on iPhones or iPads in the European Union, marking the company’s latest standoff with the continent’s antitrust watchdog.

stories.title: Incredible Interest in Space This Year: Chanin
stories.summary: Andrew Chanin, co-founder and CEO of ProcureAM, joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the SpaceX IP0 and how it has inspired investor interest in the Space ecosystem. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Jefferies Returns From Caracas Saying Dollar Flows May Lift Debt
stories.summary: Venezuela’s economy is poised for an influx of foreign investment that could support higher bond prices, according to a Wall Street analyst who visited the country last week.

stories.title: Oil prices ease after Iran says military operations against Israel are over
stories.summary: Oil rises on Monday amid heightened tensions in the Middle East, raising concerns that the conflict may drag on.

stories.title: What Apple's 2025 iPhone App of the Year Founders Want From Siri
stories.summary: As Apple kicks off WWDC in Cupertino, the spotlight is turning to inclusive design. Melissa Azari and Helene Nørlem are the co-founders of Denmark-based Tiimo, a visual planning app that was crowned Apple's iPhone App of the Year. They join Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" from Cupertino, California. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Developers Hope for Big Leaps From Apple's AI
stories.summary: Paul Hudson is a software developer and the creator of Hacking with Swift, a platform that trains people to develop on the Apple ecosystem. He joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" from in Cupertino to discuss why he is hoping for some "great APIs" from Apple. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Netanyahu says war with Iran, Hezbollah isn't over after Tehran says it's halting strikes
stories.summary: Iran and Israel traded strikes Sunday night for the first time since the U.S. and Iran agreed to a fragile ceasefire in mid-April.

stories.summary: ARM-Harith Infrastructure Investments Ltd., a private equity fund manager, said it aims to raise $200 million to invest in energy and climate infrastructure projects across sub-Saharan Africa.

stories.title: Xi Pledges Deeper Ties With Kim While Avoiding Nuclear Issue
stories.summary: President Xi Jinping vowed to expand China’s trade, agriculture and technology cooperation with North Korea, while avoiding public mentions of its neighbor’s nuclear program in a meeting with Kim Jong Un in Pyongyang.

stories.title: JPMorgan Shops 15% Yield Debt for Trump-Boosted Oil Driller
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is in talks with investors to refinance a nearly $1 billion loan at a 15% interest rate for Sable Offshore Corp., a Trump administration-supported oil driller, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Food supply 'not at risk' after new Texas screwworm cases, USDA secretary says
stories.summary: The U.S. will lean on the same playbook it did starting in the late 1950s, part of which involves releasing sterile insects to suppress the pest's population.

stories.title: Kalshi traders say Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal before January
stories.summary: Two weeks ago traders on Kalshi saw a 60% chance that the Strait of Hormuz would open before August. Now traders see a higher chance it won't open before 2027.

stories.title: SpaceX IPO Is Well Oversubscribed With $10 Billion Orders
stories.summary: SpaceX’s initial public offering is well oversubscribed with multiple institutional investors placing orders for about $10 billion or more of shares, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand builds for a potentially record-setting debut.
